page: UpCloud GPU-8xCPU-64GB-1xL40S is a GPU-accelerated virtual server powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 9575F processor with 8 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and a 2.0 GHz clock speed on the x86_64 architecture. The system is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB per core. Graphics and compute acceleration are delivered via a single NVIDIA L40S GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, which includes 48 GB of VRAM. Local storage is not bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark data reveals top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores ranking in the top 10%. Single-threaded operations such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also achieve top-tier results. Conversely, multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded decompression fall into the average tier, which is a notable tradeoff of the shared CPU allocation. Memory performance is highly competitive, with the PassMark memory score reaching the top 10% and large-block read-only memory bandwidth ranking in the top 25%.

The integrated NVIDIA L40S GPU enables strong performance in machine learning workloads. LLM inference benchmarks show top-tier text generation speeds for small 135M models and strong generation speeds for medium 7B models. However, prompt processing for large 70B models is a bottleneck, falling into the bottom 25%, though text generation for large models remains average. This server provides a resource-dense profile for GPU-accelerated tasks, making it highly suitable for small-to-medium LLM inference, image processing, and single-threaded analytical workloads.
description: UpCloud GPU-8xCPU-64GB-1xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server utilizing the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features an 8-core AMD EPYC 9575F CPU with shared allocation, 64.0 GB of system memory, and a single NVIDIA L40S GPU with 48 GB of VRAM. Local storage is not included. Benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core CPU and memory performance, alongside strong text generation speeds for small and medium language models. However, multi-core CPU performance is average, and prompt processing for large 70B models is weak. This server offers a resource-dense profile for GPU-accelerated tasks, making it highly suitable for small-to-medium LLM inference, image processing, and single-threaded analytical workloads.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-8xCPU-64GB-1xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server featuring an 8-core AMD EPYC CPU, 64 GB of RAM, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU with 48 GB of VRAM, optimized for medium LLM inference and image processing.
meta_description: UpCloud GPU-8xCPU-64GB-1xL40S is a KVM-based server with an 8-core AMD EPYC CPU, 64 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU with 48 GB VRAM.
tagline: A resource-dense GPU server combining top-tier single-core CPU performance with 48 GB of VRAM for accelerated machine learning workloads.
bullet_points: Equipped with 1 NVIDIA L40S GPU featuring 48 GB of VRAM.
Powered by an 8-core AMD EPYC 9575F CPU with 64 GB of system memory.
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance.
No local storage is bundled, requiring external storage solutions.
Best suited for small-to-medium LLM inference and image processing.
